subject: English
title: The Lost Child – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The story highlights the psychological journey of a child at a fair
The child’s fascination with material attractions represents childhood innocence
The turning point occurs when the child realizes he has lost his parents
His fear and helplessness emphasize the importance of emotional security
The story ends with the child rejecting all attractions and crying only for his parents
characters: The Lost Child – Represents innocence and emotional vulnerability
Parents – Symbolize safety, love, and guidance
The Stranger – Represents human kindness and social compassion
themes: Contrast between material desire and emotional need
literary_devices: Symbolism – The fair symbolizes temptation and confusion
Pathos – The child’s cries create emotional sympathy
Contrast – Joyful excitement changes to fear and anxiety
exam_tips: Discuss the symbolic meaning of the fair
Explain the emotional transformation of the child
Analyse the contrast between material attraction and parental love
Use textual examples of the child refusing sweets, balloons, and rides
